title: Throwing Errors ⚾️
description: How a function officially warns Swift that a failure occurred.

slides.title: The Warning Label ⚠️

quizData.question: Where exactly does the `throws` keyword go in a function declaration?

options.text: Immediately after the parameter list (), right before the return arrow ->.

quizData.explanation: Example: `func run() throws -> Int`. It acts as a massive bright red warning label right in the function signature.

slides.title: Throwing the Error ⚾️

quizData.question: If an `if` statement successfully evaluates and triggers a `throw Error` statement, what happens to the code below it?

options.text: The code completely stops, and the function forcefully exits exactly like a 'return'.

quizData.explanation: Throwing an error is the ultimate panic button. It immediately terminates the entire function runtime and ejects the error to whatever called the function.
